title: Medical Science Liaison CNS, Migraine & Biosimilars
description:
**Position Overview**


The MSL reports to the Country Medical Director. The MSL provides medical support, engages with Scientific Leaders (SLs) / Key Decision Makers (KDMs), and drives scientific initiatives.

 **Primary Responsibilities**

* Communicate scientific therapy needs credibly, appropriately, and effectively across a variety of internal and external stakeholders at all levels.
* Build trust with external scientific community via peer\-to\-peer scientific exchange, research and educational collaborations, interactions with scientific societies and health organizations, publications, etc.
* Help execute country medical educational programs and symposia.
* Support Medical advisors in conducting Advisory Boards and Expert Input Forums in line with strategy.
* Participate in and contribute significantly to professional organizations, academic or regulatory working teams.
* Provide scientific inputs for digital initiatives.

 **Job Requirements Skills**  

**(professional and/or technical)**

* Excellent communication skills (verbal, written, presentations) and interpersonal skills.
* Knowledge of local regulatory and clinical research guidelines.
* Strong prioritization skills and ability to understand how decisions fit into the broader corporate strategies.

**Leadership Behaviors**

* **INSPIRES GROWTH**
* Creating an environment that values growth and individual strengths, coaching and bringing up others to drive engagement and support career development.

* **COMMUNICATES WITH OPTIMISM AND AUTHENTICITY**
* Cultivating an engaged, mission driven workplace with transparency and empathy that differentiates Organon leaders.

* **INNOVATES WITH AGILITY**
* Having an entrepreneurial mindset that elevates performance through new and improved ways of working. Enabling decision\-making while understanding risk and ensuring progress over perfection.

* **DELIVERS WITH ACCOUNTABILITY**
* Taking ownership in setting and effectively delivering stretch goals in a sustainable and ethical way, always rising together to the challenge when problems and opportunities appear

* **ACTIVATES BELONGING**
* Driving a culture of belonging and an environment of psychological safety, fueled by curiosity of difference and grounded in optimism and respect**.**

**Experience**

* Minimum 1\-2 years Medical Science Liaison experience (preferentially)
* Clinical and/or research experience in therapeutic area of interest (preferred)

**Education / Certificates**

* Medical degree, Pharm D or Pharmacist (Pharm D equivalent), Biologist with PhD

**Secondary Job Description**

**Who We Are:**

Organon delivers ingenious health solutions that enable people to live their best lives. We are a $6\.5 billion global healthcare company focused on making a world of difference for women, their families and the communities they care for. We have an important portfolio and are growing it by investing in the unmet needs of Women’s Health, expanding access to leading biosimilars and touching lives with a diverse and trusted portfolio of health solutions. Our Vision is clear: A better and healthier every day for every woman.


As an equal opportunity employer, we welcome applications from candidates with a diverse background. We are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all our applicants.

**Search Firm Representatives Please Read Carefully**  

Organon LLC, does not accept unsolicited assistance from search firms for employment opportunities. All CVs / resumes submitted by search firms to any employee at our company without a valid written search agreement in place for this position will be deemed the sole property of our company. No fee will be paid in the event a candidate is hired by our company as a result of an agency referral where no pre\-existing agreement is in place. Where agency agreements are in place, introductions are position specific. Please, no phone calls or emails.

 **The annual base salary range listed below reflects the expected pay for new hires in this role across each applicable location**. Final salary determinations take several factors into account including, but not limited to, primary work location, relevant skills, education level, and/or prior work experience. In addition to base salary, this position may be eligible for an annual bonus and participation in a long\-term incentive program, depending on the role level. Further details about the full compensation and benefits package will be provided during the recruitment process. Salary ranges for multiple locations may be displayed to comply with pay transparency laws and because these locations may serve as potential work sites for the role. If a specific location or country does not show a salary range, compensation details will be discussed during the hiring process.

  

For this role:

Site Activation Specialist
description:
**Site Activation Specialist**


**Country Site Activation \- CSA**


**Italy**


**Make an impact on patient health!**


IQVIA’s Global Site Activation Team of 2000 employees, drives best in class site and customer experience, with industry leading outcomes. We keep the patient at the forefront; ‘Always Impatient for the Patient’ and we excel in our collaboration with our external/internal customers.


IQVIA’s Country Site Activation team are a global network who identify and activate sites to the highest quality to ensure patients on Clinical trials have access to safe and effective medication. We are leaders in innovation and first to new technologies, meaning a vast amount of career development opportunities are available.


**Job Overview:**


As a Site Activation Specialist, you will serve as a Single Point of Contact to perform feasibility, site activation and some maintenance activities in assigned studies for investigative sites, according to applicable regulations, SOPs and work instructions, working closely with the Site Activation Manager, Project Management team, and other departments as necessary.


You will review documents for completeness, consistency, and accuracy, under guidance of senior staff and prepare site documents, inform team members of completion of regulatory contracts. Along with distributing completed documents to sites and internal project team member and updating and maintaining internal systems, databases, tracking tools, timelines, and project plans with accurate and complete project specific information.


You will also review, track, and follow up the progress, the approval and execution of required documents such as questionnaires, CDAs, regulatory, ethics, Informed Consent Form, and Investigator Pack release documents.


**Requirements and Skills :**

* Bachelor’s Degree in Life Science
* 2 \- 3 years’ prior experience in clinical research / site activation
* Experience of regulatory submissions essential.
* Ability to work independently but also collaborate with others
* Leadership skills
* Good interpersonal, communication and organisational skills to establish and maintain effective working relationships with co\-workers, managers and sponsors
* Ability to work on multiple projects
* Knowledge of applicable functional/regulatory requirements, including local regulations, SOPs and ICH/ GCP

**Join IQVIA and let’s create a healthier world together:**


* Exposure across 21 Therapeutic Areas
* Be part of a global team who operate across 141 locations
* Exposure across a variety of studies and projects
* Access to new, innovative technologies
* Work within a collaborative and vibrant environment
* Own your career!

**Unleash your potential!**


It takes passion to make the extraordinary possible for patients. Our culture of innovation and collaboration enables us to explore new possibilities and help improve health around the world. When you join our diverse, global team, you’ll harness the power of unparalleled data, advanced analytics, cutting\-edge technologies, and deep healthcare and scientific expertise to drive healthcare forward.


IQVIA is a leading global provider of clinical research services, commercial insights and healthcare intelligence to the life sciences and healthcare industries. We create intelligent connections to accelerate the development and commercialization of innovative medical treatments to help improve patient outcomes and population health worldwide. Learn more at https://jobs.iqvia.com


IQVIA is committed to integrity in our hiring process and maintains a zero tolerance policy for candidate fraud. All information and credentials submitted in your application must be truthful and complete. Any false statements, misrepresentations, or material omissions during the recruitment process will result in immediate disqualification of your application, or termination of employment if discovered later, in accordance with applicable law. We appreciate your honesty and professionalism.


The potential base pay range for this role, when annualized, is €33,000\.00 \- €49,400\.00\. The actual base pay offered may vary based on a number of factors including job\-related qualifications such as knowledge, skills, education, and experience; location; and/or schedule (full or part\-time). Dependent on the position offered, incentive plans, bonuses, and/or other forms of compensation may be offered, in addition to a range of health and welfare and/or other benefits.
company_description: IQVIA (NYSE:IQV) is a leading global provider of advanced analytics, technology solutions and clinical research services to the life sciences industry dedicated to delivering actionable insights.

title: Operations Project Manager
description:
PURPOSE
MedTech AI \- Operations


LOCATION
Rome



Description
The Operations Project Manager supports the Director of Operational Excellence and Vice President of Operations with the development and operation of the Project Management Office (PMO), and leads projects and programmes across the R\&D Factory and cross\-functionally as required. This role is a hands\-on delivery position, executing projects using PMI based PMO standards and ensuring disciplined planning, risk management, and reporting in support of Cosmo’s Operational Excellence strategy. The role works in close partnership with the Director of Operational Excellence and the Vice President of Operations, acting as a key architect of a high\-performance, disciplined, and resilient project management execution.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES* **PMO Execution \& Governance Support**  

Support the development, deployment, and continuous improvement of PMO processes and tools  

Execute projects using standardised governance, reporting, and risk frameworks.  

Maintain project plans, schedules, dependencies, and delivery dashboards.
* **Project \& Programme Delivery**  

Lead R\&D\-focused projects, ensuring effective cross\-functional collaboration with Manufacturing, Service, and Supply Chain when required.  

Manage timelines, resources, deliverables, and interdependencies.  

Drive disciplined execution in line with approved plans and priorities.
* **Risk, Issue \& Dependency Management**  

Proactively identify project risks, issues, and dependencies.  

Drive mitigation actions and escalation through PMO governance.  

Support recovery planning and execution where delivery risks materialise.
* **Stakeholder Coordination**  

Coordinate cross\-functional teams and stakeholders across the R\&D Factory.  

Ensure clarity of roles, responsibilities, and expectations for all project participants.  

Support alignment between functional teams and programme objectivies.
* **Reporting \& Performance Visibility**  

Provide clear, structured project and programme reporting to Operations \& R\&D Factory leadership.  

Support portfolio level visibility and decision\-making.  

Ensure transparency of execution status, risks, and mitigation actions.
* **Support to Operational Excellence Initiatives**  

Support Operational Excellence initiatives driven by the Director of Operational Excellence.  

Contribute to the refinement of PMO standards, tools, and ways of working.  

Support rollout of best practices across Operations, R\&D Factory, and cross\-functional teams.
* **Continuous Improvement \& Learning**  

Capture lessons learned and best practices from all projects and programmes.  

Support continuous improvement of delivery discipline and execution quality.  

Promote a culture of accountability, structure, and disciplined execution.

QUALIFICATIONS, SKILLS AND ABILITIES* Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Life Sciences, Computer Science, or related field.
* 5\+ years of experience in project or program management within medical devices, digital health, or other regulated technology domains.
* Strong understanding of structured product development processes, including design control and risk management.
* Experience coordinating cross\-functional teams spanning engineering, quality, regulatory, and operations.
* Excellent communication, organization, and stakeholder\-management skills.
* Ability to manage multiple programs concurrently in a dynamic and evolving environment.

OTHER REQUIREMENTS* Experience managing programs involving Software as a Medical Device (SaMD), AI\-enabled technologies, or medical electrical equipment.
* Familiarity with EU MDR, FDA pathways, and quality system expectations (ISO 13485\).
* Experience working in customer\-facing or contract\-engineering environments.
* PMP or equivalent project management certification.

* **Salary Package:**  

**Annual Gross Salary (RAL):** €50,000 – €55,000 based on experience.  

**Level and Contract: F**ixed\-term contract (CCNL Commercio).  

**Others:** 2 days/week of smart working.

APPLICATION
Email us with your CV, résumé or portfolio, and a cover letter explaining your motivation for applying. Please include the job title in your email subject line.


Please review our privacy notice for job applicants


Thanking you for your application and for sending your CV, pursuant to art. 13 and 14 of the General Data Protection Regulation (EU) 2016/679, we provide you with all the necessary information relating to the processing of your personal data. The processed data will be your personal data that you deem appropriate to include in your CV. We invite you not to provide in your CV Personal Data falling within the special category of Personal Data pursuant to art. 9 of EU Regulation no. 679/2016(such as, for example, racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious or philosophical beliefs, union membership, data relating to the person's health or sex life or sexual orientation). We therefore reserve the right to delete or make anonymous such data if it is entered anyway. We inform you that your personal data will be processed in order to carry out an objective assessment of your professional and personal requirements in relation to the possibility of hiring for an open position, or future contact in the event of the opening of new positions relating to your professional figure. In compliance with the Art. 6 of the Regulation, the Data Processing is necessary to take steps at the request of the data subject prior to entering into a contract. The personal data being processed are provided directly by you; during an interview, the Data Controller may collect further data, including particular data, collected directly from you or from other sources; personal data from public registers may also constitute processing, always for purposes connected with the correct management of the employment relationship; during the interview you will be given specific information. All the aforementioned data will be kept for a period of 1 year and only in the event of the establishment of the employment relationship will they be kept for the entire duration of the contract and in relation to the terms established by the relevant legal obligations. Without prejudice to communications made in compliance with legal obligations, all data collected and processed may be communicated to categories of subjects such as the personnel office, function or area managers, group companies and labor consultants. The rights enshrined in the Regulation (access, rectification, cancellation, restriction and opposition, portability, and the right to lodge a complaint with the Authority) may be exercised against the Data Controller by writing to the following e\-mail address: legal@cosmoimd.com For more information, please view the complete privacy policy at the link: cosmohealthconfidence/privacy\-policy

title: Product Sales Expert – Central Laboratory Solutions, Italy (m/f/d)
description:
Join us in pioneering breakthroughs in healthcare. For everyone. Everywhere. Sustainably.


Our inspiring and caring environment forms a global community that celebrates diversity and individuality. We encourage you to step beyond your comfort zone, offering resources and flexibility to foster your professional and personal growth, all while valuing your unique contributions.


Apply now for the position of **Product Sales Expert – Central Laboratory Solutions** (CLS) within our Diagnostics Sales organization. In this role, you will have the unique opportunity to work alongside a team of technical and scientific experts in the fields of Automation, Clinical Chemistry, and Immunoassay. You will provide specialized technical consulting, capture customer requirements and designing tailored solutions by leveraging the Siemens Healthineers CLS product portfolio.


We are currently seeking candidates for **two positions**: one based in **Northern Italy** and one **in Southern Italy.**

**Your role:**

**Customer Engagement \& Relationship Building**

* Drive customer satisfaction by regularly visiting clients to monitor their needs, uncover new business opportunities, and continuously improve their experience in close synergy with the sales team.
* Cultivate strong networks with laboratory managers and Key Opinion Leaders (KOLs) to position our brand as a trusted industry benchmark.

**Strategic Collaboration \& Solution Design**

* Analyze complex environments (including workflows and deal strategies) to map out and design tailored, cutting\-edge solutions that perfectly meet customer needs.
* Partner cross\-functionally with Territory Sales, Marketing, Project Management, and Consulting Solutions to turn funnel analysis into actionable business opportunities.
* Prioritize activities aligned with the sales pipeline, crafting joint action plans with Territory Managers and engaging internal functions to ensure flawless execution.

**Commercial \& Technical Excellence**

* Deliver impactful presentations that highlight our products' unique value proposition and key market differentiators.
* Shape winning proposals for public tenders and private negotiations, contributing to commercial offers with a focus on analytical configurations and consumables.
* Act as a key resource by providing strategic technical support during dispute and litigation scenarios, while continuously monitoring market dynamics and competitive intelligence.
* Empower the sales force by contributing to their ongoing training, coaching, and professional development.
* Maintain project momentum through detailed progress reporting aligned with internal stakeholder timelines.


For High\-Complexity Projects, you will:

* Master Value Selling: design and deliver impactful solutions leveraging the Value Selling Methodology (VSM).
* Bring solutions to life: create advanced technical layouts using modern design tools such as CAD, Visio, and 3D software.
* Unleash innovation: maximize the potential of our advanced portfolio, integrating Automation, AI, Digital IT, Business Intelligence, and Robotics into game\-changing projects.

**Your expertise:**

* Experienced professional with at least 3\-5 years of similar previous expertise in the Diagnostics sector, specifically in Core Lab Solutions, including Immunochemistry, Chemistry and Automation.
* Demonstrated expertise in cross\-functional environments, providing commercial expertise and innovative solutions to meet customer needs.
* Office Package knowledge
* Fluent in English written and spoken
* Availability to travel (up to 50% of working time) within the Italian country
* Excellent communication and presentation skills
* Ability to thrive in complex organizational settings with matrix structure.

**Compensation \& Benefits**


In accordance with our commitment to pay transparency and equal opportunity, we provide a clear breakdown of the financial package for this role:

* The position is regulated by the Chemical\-Pharmaceutical National Collective Labor Agreement (CCNL Chimico\-Farmaceutico), qualifica Impiegato.
* The base salary for this position typically ranges from € 44\.500 \- 59\.500 (RAL). The final offer will be tailored based on your individual skills, experience and specific location.
* Performance Bonus: this role is eligible for a variable incentive plan linked to specific achievements (minimum 10%).
* Total Rewards: beyond the base pay, we offer a comprehensive benefits package including flexible working, health insurance, and pension scheme. We are deeply committed to your growth, providing continuous learning opportunities with a dedicated budget for both technical advancement and soft skills development.


To find out more about the specific business, have a look at ***www.siemens\-healthineers.com.***

**Who we are:**  



  

We are a team of more than 71,000 highly dedicated Healthineers in more than 70 countries. As a leader in medical technology, we constantly push the boundaries to create better outcomes and experiences for patients, no matter where they live or what health issues they are facing. Our portfolio is crucial for clinical decision\-making and treatment pathways.

**How we work:**  



Siemens Healthineers values diversity and ensures equal opportunities for all candidates, regardless of gender, gender identity and expression, sexual orientation, age, ethnic and national origin, religion or belief, disability and health conditions, marital and family status, pregnancy, and maternity/paternity. The selection process is conducted in compliance with the principle of non\-discrimination pursuant to Legislative Decree 198/2006 (Equal Opportunities Code), Legislative Decree 216/2003, and applicable European legislation. Applications are evaluated solely based on the skills, experience, and aptitudes required for the position. At no stage of the recruitment process will candidates be required to provide information regarding their current or past remuneration.


We care about your data privacy and take compliance with GDPR as well as other data protection legislation seriously. For this reason, we ask you not to send us your CV or resume by email. Please **create a profile** within our talent community and subscribe to personalized job alerts that will keep you posted about new opportunities.

**To all recruitment agencies:**


Siemens Healthineers' recruitment is internally managed, with external support permitted only when a qualified supplier has established a formal contract with us. Unsolicited candidate submissions and referrals, absent a current supplier contract, do not establish consent and are ineligible for fees. We delete and destroy unsolicited information, thus, would recommend you refrain from any such practices. Your adherence to our policies is appreciated.

title: Inside Sales Specialist Roma
description:
**Date:** Jul 14, 2026


**Location:** Rome, IT, 00185 Italy Home Office, IT, NA
**Company:** Dentsply Sirona, Inc


**Requistion ID**: 83406  


  

Dentsply Sirona is the world’s largest manufacturer of professional dental products and technologies, with over a century of innovation and service to the dental industry and patients worldwide. Dentsply Sirona develops, manufactures, and markets a comprehensive solutions offering including dental and oral health products as well as other consumable medical devices under a strong portfolio of world class brands.  



Dentsply Sirona’s products provide innovative, high\-quality, and effective solutions to advance patient care and deliver better and safer dental care. Dentsply Sirona’s headquarter is located in Charlotte, North Carolina. The company’s shares are listed in the United States on NASDAQ under the symbol XRAY. Visit www.dentsplysirona.com for more information about Dentsply Sirona and its products.  



  

**Inside Sales Specialist**

  



For our new offices in Rome (Cinecittà area), we are looking for an **Inside Sales Specialist** for a **fixed\-term contract (12 months)**.



The role is responsible for driving profitable and sustainable growth of the Consumables business, primarily across **Restorative, Preventive, Endodontic and Laboratory Solutions**, by generating demand, identifying new business opportunities, reactivating dormant customers, and supporting sales conversion through remote interactions with dental clinics, distributors and assigned accounts.

  


**Responsibilities**

  


* Conduct consultative sales activities by phone with existing customers, acquire new customers/prospects, and reactivate inactive accounts.
* Utilize the CRM system (Salesforce), including documenting customer interactions (virtual Customer Facing Events) and entering transfer orders for dealers.
* Support dealers during commercial campaigns and collaborate with the Field Sales team by generating leads and contributing to revenue growth.
* Support special sales initiatives and digital promotional campaigns.
* Actively manage an assigned customer portfolio based on product line or geographical area, developing long\-term commercial relationships. The sales process includes:
+ Initiating and developing customer relationships to effectively manage and close sales opportunities.
+ Identifying cross\-selling and upselling opportunities across product lines.
+ Promoting strategic product offerings in line with company priorities.
+ Maintaining accurate records of sales activities and customer interactions within Salesforce CRM.
+ Identifying cross\-selling opportunities across different product lines and business units.
+ Contributing to the overall commercial strategy.
+ Keeping up to date with market trends, competitor activities and new product launches, providing insights and recommendations.
+ Collaborating effectively with distribution partners throughout the sales process.
+ Building and maintaining strong relationships both internally (Field Sales team) and externally (dealers), ensuring continuity and customer engagement beyond the initial sale.

The assigned customer portfolio may remain stable or evolve over time according to business needs.

  


**Required Profile**

  


* Educational background in Business, Healthcare or Dentistry, with experience in Sales, Marketing or Communication.
* Minimum of **3 years of sales experience**.
* Experience using virtual/digital selling techniques and digital collaboration tools.
* Proficiency in Microsoft Office and CRM systems (Salesforce preferred).
* Interest in technical and medical topics, with strong attention to detail and follow\-up skills.
* Excellent verbal and written communication, organizational and interpersonal skills.
* Native\-level Italian.
* Good written and spoken English.
* Spanish is considered a plus.

  


**Competencies**

  


* Accountability
* Negotiation and decision\-making skills
* Active listening
* Assertiveness
* Analytical thinking, planning and prioritization skills
* Teamwork and collaboration
* Customer and results orientation
* Adaptability in a fast\-changing environment
* Complex problem\-solving skills
* Resilience

  


**What We Offer**


* Fixed annual gross salary of **€26,000**, plus a **performance\-based bonus of up to 30% of the annual gross salary**.
* Meal vouchers.
* Private health insurance.
* Employee Assistance Program (EAP).
* Hybrid working after the initial onboarding and training period.
* Access to learning, development and personal growth programs.

  

  

**Equal Opportunity Employer:** Dentsply Sirona is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will be considered without unlawful discrimination or regard for race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, sexual or gender identity, national or ethnic origin, age, marital status, disability, genetic factors, military and veteran status, or any other characteristics protected by applicable local law.
**Eligibility:** All successful applicants must be eligible to work in the country the position is based.
**Assistance:** If you need assistance with completing the online application due to a disability, please send an accommodation request to accommodationrequest@dentsplysirona.com.
**Agencies:** Please note that Dentsply Sirona does not accept or respond to unsolicited requests or applications submitted by Recruitment Agencies/ Search Firms.
**Notice on Fraudulent Job Offers:** Unfortunately, we are aware of third parties that pretend to represent our company offering unauthorized employment opportunities. If you think a fraudulent source is offering you a job, please have a look at the following information: careers.dentsplysirona.com.

Therapy Specialist - TAVI - Sud Italia
description:
About Abbott  

At Abbott, we’re committed to helping people live their best possible life through the power of health. For over 125 years, we’ve been developing innovative technologies and products across diagnostics, medical devices, nutrition, and pharmaceuticals—reaching people in more than 150 countries.  

Join us and be part of a global team of 99,000\+ professionals making a real impact on patients’ lives every day.

  

The Opportunity  

We are looking for a Junior Clinical Specialist to join our Structural Heart team, focusing on TAVI (Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation).  

This is an exciting opportunity for someone at the beginning of their career to work side by side with clinicians, supporting life\-changing procedures and gaining hands\-on experience in a highly innovative field.  

You will play a key role in supporting healthcare professionals during procedures, building strong clinical partnerships, and contributing to improved patient outcomes.

  

What You’ll Do  

* Provide on\-site clinical and technical support during TAVI procedures in cath labs and operating rooms
* Build strong relationships with cardiologists, cardiac surgeons, and clinical staff
* Support the coordination of procedures and ensure smooth collaboration across teams
* Deliver training sessions to healthcare professionals on TAVI technology and best practices
* Present clinical data and product value to referring physicians
* Contribute to achieving business and performance objectives in your area
* Ensure full compliance with company policies and healthcare regulations
**Preferred location: Palermo**

  

What We’re Looking For  

* Degree in Biomedical Engineering or a related field
* 0–2 years of experience (internships or first experience in medical devices is a plus)
* Strong interest in clinical environments and patient care
* Good level of English (written and spoken)
* Excellent communication and presentation skills
* Proactive, curious, and willing to learn in a fast\-paced environment
* Availability to travel within the Lazio region

Why Join Abbott?  

* Work on cutting\-edge structural heart technologies
* Gain hands\-on experience in the operating room
* Continuous learning and development opportunities
* Be part of a purpose\-driven company that improves lives

title: Principal Research Scientist
description:
Ref. 208 S
  
  

Principal Research Scientist
  
  

Structural Biology Unit
  
  

IRBM is a scientific contract research organization (CRO) that stands at the forefront of drug discovery, leveraging a single, unified research facility, located in Pomezia (Rome), Italy, to accelerate team\-based breakthroughs and improve efficiencies from initial concept to pre\-clinical candidate.
  
  

With a history of contributing to several approved drugs, and its own internal pipeline of oncology and other therapeutic assets, IRBM is powered by deep collaborations and decades of expertise from leading global institutions. Our comprehensive in\-house capabilities assist partners in achieving key milestones and progressing towards clinical readiness, positioning IRBM as a major player in driving innovation in the pharmaceutical and biotech fields.
  
  

IRBM is a scientific Contract Research Organization (CRO) that stands at the forefront of drug discovery, leveraging a single, unified research facility, located in Pomezia (Rome), Italy, to accelerate team\-based breakthroughs and improve efficiencies from initial concept to pre\-clinical candidate.
  
  

With a history of contributing to several approved drugs, and its own internal pipeline of oncology and other therapeutic assets, IRBM is powered by deep collaborations and decades of expertise from leading global institutions. Our comprehensive in\-house capabilities assist partners in achieving key milestones and progressing towards clinical readiness, positioning IRBM as a major player in driving innovation in the pharmaceutical and biotech fields.
  
  

Job Description
  
  

We are seeking a talented, highly motivated Crystallographer with a strong passion for structural biology and structure\-based drug discovery. The successful candidate will join a rapidly expanding Protein Production and NMR team and contribute to a collaborative, multidisciplinary research environment focused on advancing drug discovery projects involving proteins, nucleic acids, peptides, and small\-molecule ligands. The ideal candidate will have a solid background in macromolecular crystallography and a demonstrated track record of delivering high\-quality scientific results. She/He should possess extensive training in structural biology, strong analytical and problem\-solving skills, and a creative, innovative approach to addressing complex scientific challenges. A commitment to continuous learning and scientific excellence is essential. The role requires hands\-on expertise in protein crystallization, diffraction data collection, structure determination, and structural analysis. The successful applicant will work closely with scientists across multiple disciplines to generate high\-impact structural insights that guide drug discovery and biomolecular research programs.
  
  

Key responsibilities
  
  

Lead and execute crystal structure determination projects using X\-ray crystallography, electron diffraction, and complementary structural biology techniques
  
  

Independently analyze, interpret, integrate and communicate experimental data and structural insights to internal project teams, external collaborators, and clients
  
  

Design, develop, and optimize experimental strategies to address challenging crystallographic and structure determination problems
  
  

Maintain accurate experimental records and contribute to laboratory operations, including reagent procurement, inventory management, and equipment oversight
  
  

Provide scientific leadership and intellectual input to structure\-based drug discovery programs, working closely with multidisciplinary teams spanning medicinal chemistry, biology, biophysics, computational chemistry, and related fields.
  
  

Generate and apply structural insights to support target validation, hit identification, lead optimization, and candidate selection in multidisciplinary drug discovery projects.
  
  

Contribute to the continuous improvement of crystallography workflows, methodologies, and data analysis pipelines
  
  

Contribute to scientific publications, scientific reports, technical proposals, and business development activities as appropriate.
  
  

Competencies/skills
  
  

Extensive expertise in macromolecular crystallography, including X\-ray diffraction, electron diffraction, and related structural biology techniques
  
  

Strong proficiency with crystallographic software packages, data processing and structure determination tools, and relevant scripting or programming languages for data analysis and automation
  
  

Demonstrated hands\-on experience in protein crystallization, crystal optimization, high\-throughput crystallography, synchrotron\-based data collection and automated data collection workflows
  
  

Proven ability to work both independently and collaboratively in a fast\-paced research environment, while effectively managing multiple projects and meeting timelines
  
  

Experience mentoring and guiding junior scientists, with the ability to provide scientific and technical leadership when required
  
  

Excellent organizational skills, attention to detail, critical thinking, and creative problem\-solving abilities
  
  

Strong interpersonal skills with a highly collaborative and team\-oriented mindset, coupled with outstanding written and verbal communication skills
  
  

Fluency in spoken and written English is essential
  
  

Qualifications
  
  

Candidates should hold a Ph.D. in Structural Biology, Chemistry, or a related field, complemented by a minimum of five years of relevant postdoctoral and/or industry experience in macromolecular crystallography. Additional experience with cryo\-electron microscopy (cryo\-EM) techniques and/or Protein Production will be considered as a plus.
  
  

The position is based in Pomezia (RM).
  
  

Salary Pay Range
  
  

RAL € 38\.000 to € 45\.000 – CCNL Chimico Farmaceutico

title: Electronical Process Engineer
description:
**Job Requisition No**

 VN8476  

**Work Location Name**

 Italy \- Rome  

**Worker Basis**

 Full Time  

**Worker Type**

 Employee  

**Contract Type**

 Permanent  

**Applications Close Date**

 Dec 31, 2026  

**Purpose**


As a **Process Engineer** within BVI Medical’s Capital Equipment Operations team, you will be responsible for support the development, transfer, validation, and continuous improvement of manufacturing and testing processes for ophthalmic surgical consoles and related electro\-mechanical medical devices.  

  

In this role, you will work in a dynamic and international environment, collaborating closely with R\&D, Technology Transfer, Manufacturing, Quality, Supply Chain, Sourcing, and external technical partners. You will contribute to the industrialization of new products, support lifecycle management initiatives, and drive process improvements aimed at enhancing quality, efficiency, capacity, and cost\-effectiveness.  

  

This position is ideal for an engineer with strong technical expertise in electronics, electrical systems, or mechatronics, who is passionate about solving complex engineering challenges and supporting the development of innovative medical devices for ophthalmic surgery.  

  

**Key Responsibilities**

* Develop, implement, and validate manufacturing and testing processes for ophthalmic surgical consoles and capital equipment.
* Support New Product Introduction activities, ensuring a smooth transfer from design and prototyping phases to stable manufacturing processes, ensuring robust, scalable, and compliant processes.
* Provide technical support for electro\-mechanical assemblies, electrical wiring, PCBA integration, functional testing, and system\-level troubleshooting.
* Collaborate with R\&D, Technology Transfer, Manufacturing, Quality, Supply Chain, Sourcing, and other Operations functions to define and execute optimal industrialization plans.
* Act as the voice of Operations within the product development cycle, supporting Design for Manufacturing, Design for Testability, and Design for Excellence reviews.
* Define and maintain process documentation, including work instructions, process flows, test methods, validation protocols and reports, PFMEAs, and control plans.
* Conduct process studies and data analysis to identify opportunities for improving quality, process robustness, capacity, cycle time, and cost performance.
* Lead or support root cause analysis, corrective and preventive actions, and technical investigations related to process or product issues, .
* Support the introduction, qualification, and validation of new manufacturing technologies, tools, fixtures, and test equipment.
* Ensure manufacturing processes are compliant with applicable quality system requirements and medical device regulations.
* Provide training to production operators on new processes and operating procedures, as well as updates to existing procedures.
* Interface with internal and external suppliers, manufacturers, and technical partners to support process development, tooling, equipment qualification, prototyping, and production readiness.
  

  

**Qualification**

* Master’s degree in Electronics Engineering, Mechatronics Engineering, Biomedical Engineering.
* Minimum 8 years of relevant experience in process engineering, manufacturing engineering, industrialization, product development, or a similar technical role.
* Strong technical knowledge of electronics, electrical systems, mechatronics, and electro\-mechanical assemblies.
* Knowledge of functional testing, system\-level troubleshooting, and integration of electro\-mechanical medical devices.
* Experience in process development, validation methodologies (including IQ/OQ/PQ) and continuous improvement within a manufacturing environment.
* Good understanding of electrical wiring, PCBA, functional testing, and system\-level integration of electro\-mechanical products.
* Strong engineering problem\-solving skills and ability to perform structured root cause analysis.
* Excellent analytical skills, with the ability to support data\-driven decisions and process improvements.
* Ability to work effectively in cross\-functional and international teams.
* Fluent in Italian and English.
* Experience in medical devices or other highly regulated industries will be considered a strong plus.
* Knowledge of optoelectronics, sensors, lasers, optical fibers, or medical electrical equipment will be considered a plus
* Knowledge of ISO 13485, ISO 14971, FDA 21 CFR Part 820, process validation, lean manufacturing, ECAD, or MCAD tools will be considered a plus.
* Applications from candidates belonging to protected categories under Art 1\- Law 68/99, are welcome and will be positively considered.


**Offered Salary Range:** €40,000–€45,000  

**CCNL:** Metalmeccanico Industria  

  

**Company**


BVI® is refocusing the future of vision.  

  

As one of the fastest\-growing, diversified surgical ophthalmic businesses in the world, our purpose\-built portfolio of trusted brands includes: Beaver® (Knives and Blades), Visitec® (Cannulas), Malosa® (Single\-Use Instruments), Vitreq® (Vitreoretinal Surgical Products) and PhysIOL® (Premium IOLs), and spans more than 90 countries.  

  

We’ve set our sights on touching the lives of millions of patients affected by conditions such as cataracts, refractive error, glaucoma, retinal disease, and dry eye. Unburdened by legacy or bureaucracy, we have developed our strategy around a simple concept – taking pride in delivering innovative solutions for our physicians and patients, based on their needs. We trust and empower our associates to make decisions and solve problems because collaboration drives us. Valuing agility, simplicity, and transparency, we stay committed to listening to our customers, delivering for our patients, and keeping the future in focus.  

  

Learn more at www.bvimedical.com
